sourceMovie.title: Astro Boy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Astro Boy (2009) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Titan A.E.
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Astro Boy clicked for you, Titan A.E. (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and action, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Astro Boy clicked for you, Next Avengers: Heroes of Tomorrow (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and action, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Scooby-Doo! & Batman: The Brave and the Bold
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Astro Boy clicked for you, Scooby-Doo! & Batman: The Brave and the Bold (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and action, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Astro Boy?
faq.answer: Try Titan A.E., Next Avengers: Heroes of Tomorrow, Scooby-Doo! & Batman: The Brave and the Bold—each shares DNA with Astro Boy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Astro Boy?
faq.answer: Astro Boy (2009) is best described as Animation, Action, Family, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Astro Boy worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
